items.content_html:
🎨 🌾 🔧 🪶 🌲 🏔️ 🔭 ✏️

## LETTER: Set the record straight on Tŝilhqot’in territory Published 5:30 am Tuesday, July 14, 2026 Letters to the editor for the Williams Lake Tribune can be sent to editor@wltribune.com (File photo) Editor, As Tsilhqot’in, we are connected to all the First Nations in this region, through blood, marriage, trade and history. We truly value those connections and do our best to focus on these bonds that tie us together as nenqayni (people of the land). For that reason, we would love nothing more than to celebrate the new welcome sign unveiled by Williams Lake First Nation (WLFN) and its

## 19-year-old Williams Lake man with grey, white cat reported missing Published 4:34 pm Tuesday, July 14, 2026 **By Williams Lake Tribune Staff** Joel Burdett. (BC RCMP) RCMP in Williams Lake is seeking the public’s help in locating a 19-year-old man who was reported missing Tuesday, July 14\. Joel Burdett was last seen on Monday, July 13\. He is described as a Caucasian male with a slim built, blonde hair and brown eyes. He is approximately six feet, five inches tall and was last seen wearing a red hoodie and black pants. According to a news release, Burdett is also believed to be carrying

items.title: B.C. approves tailings dam height increase at Mount Polley mine, site of massive 2014 breach : My Cariboo Now
items.content_html:
🎨 🌾 🔧 🪶 🌲 🏔️ 🔭 ✏️

British Columbia has approved a 13-metre increase to the tailings dam at the Mount Polley mine, nearly 12 years after one of Canada's worst mining disasters at the site.

## HANSEN: Inclement weather causes road damage, fire in Lac La Hache Published 5:00 pm Tuesday, July 14, 2026 **By Barbara Hansen** 1/3 A pair of firefighters from the Lac La Hache Volunteer Fire Department monitor a fire on Caverly Road on June 19, 2026\. (Photo Submitted) It sure doesn’t take long for wet, miserable weather to wreck the gravel roads. Truthfully, I think we had about two weeks of smooth driving and now, at the one-month mark, we need to see our friendly grader operator again on Spout Lake Road. Up here in the hills, we have open range and there are tons of Mamas, Daddies

## 1,200 hectare wildfire burning west of Fraser Lake Published 4:48 pm Sunday, July 12, 2026 **By Yashvika Grover** 1/2 K70748 increased fire behaviour on upslope areas of north flank July 10\. (BC Wildfire Service) A wildfire located 1.5 kilometres west of the Fraser River continues to grow. According to BC Wildfire Service (BCWS), the French Bar Creek fire (K70748) was first discovered on July 4 and the cause remains under investigation. The out-of-control blaze currently spans 1,246.3 hectares. BCWS has assigned 59 firefighters, 10 helicopters and 2 pieces of heavy equipment. BCWS said

## Quesnel Safeway opens hidden time capsule after nearly 40 years Published 10:13 am Friday, July 10, 2026 **By Laisa Conde** 1/7 A few of the items that were kept in the time capsule found at Quesnel's Safeway. (Laísa Condé/Quesnel Cariboo Observer) A dusty bucket hidden behind Quesnel’s Safeway roof for nearly four decades opened a remarkable window into life in 1987. Gloria Moskalyk, manager at Safeway, said about three years ago her office was going through some lighting upgrades. By the time the contractor finished their work one morning, they left a time capsule sitting on her desk.
